summary: Matched the Runtime Gecko_setjmp unit by replacing the standalone asm file with MWERKS_GEKKO inline assembly in src/Runtime/Gecko_setjmp.c, defining a real __jmp_buf layout in Gecko_setjmp.h, and switching build metadata from asm/Runtime/Gecko_setjmp.s.o to src/Runtime/Gecko_setjmp.c.o. The PR also propagated the new __jmp_buf type into baselib globals, replacing a placeholder 0x828-byte unknown type for hsd_804D2648.
what_changed: Deleted asm/Runtime/Gecko_setjmp.s, which contained assembly implementations of __setjmp and longjmp.
Implemented __setjmp and longjmp in src/Runtime/Gecko_setjmp.c as MWERKS_GEKKO inline asm blocks using nofralloc and register-qualified __jmp_buf pointers.
Added non-MWERKS_GEKKO fallback stubs for __setjmp and longjmp that call NOT_IMPLEMENTED, requiring placeholder.h in the C file.
Changed Runtime/Gecko_setjmp.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py.
Changed obj_files.mk to build src/Runtime/Gecko_setjmp.c.o instead of asm/Runtime/Gecko_setjmp.s.o.
Replaced placeholder Gecko_setjmp.h contents with a concrete __jmp_buf struct containing saved PC, CR, SP, RTOC, a reserved word, GPR save area for r13-r31, FP registers fp14-fp31, and FPSCR.
Updated __setjmp and longjmp prototypes to take register __jmp_buf* arguments rather than void*/UNK_T placeholder signatures.
In src/sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3A94.h, included Gecko_setjmp.h and changed hsd_804D2648 from a local placeholder __baselib_UnkType001 to __jmp_buf.
Removed a now-redundant Gecko_setjmp.h include from src/sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3B33.c because hsd_3A94.h now provides it.
smart_moves: Kept the exact register-save and restore logic visible in C source via MWERKS inline asm rather than leaving the object as a separate asm file.

Modeled the jump buffer with named fields matching the assembly offsets, letting inline asm use env->pc, env->cr, env->sp, env->rtoc, env->gprs, fp fields, and env->fpscr instead of raw numeric offsets.
Preserved the longjmp C library behavior where a requested return value of 0 becomes 1, matching the assembly sequence cmpwi/mr/mtfsf/bnelr/li.
Moved the recovered jump-buffer type into the Runtime header and reused it in baselib, reducing anonymous placeholder typing around hsd_804D2648.
Guarded compiler-specific inline assembly with MWERKS_GEKKO and supplied fallback stubs for other build contexts.
decomp_lessons: For compiler/runtime routines that are inherently register-manipulating, matching can still be advanced by converting raw .s files into MWERKS inline asm inside the C source.
Recovering a struct layout from assembly offsets is useful even when the implementation remains inline asm; field names document offset meaning and prevent magic-offset drift.
When a placeholder global's size and usage correspond to a recovered runtime structure, replace the local unknown typedef with the shared real type in the owning header.
Build metadata must be updated in both configure.py and obj_files.mk when retiring an asm object in favor of a matched C object.
Keep non-target compiler behavior explicit for MWERKS-only inline asm, typically with conditional compilation and NOT_IMPLEMENTED fallbacks.
naming_conventions: The recovered jump buffer type is named __jmp_buf, matching the Runtime/Gecko_setjmp context and C runtime convention.
Struct fields use short register/role names that mirror saved machine state: pc, cr, sp, rtoc, reserved, gprs, fp14 through fp31, and fpscr.
The public prototypes remain the runtime names __setjmp and longjmp.
The baselib global hsd_804D2648 keeps its address-derived name but now has type __jmp_buf instead of a baselib-specific unknown placeholder.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used MWERKS_GEKKO asm functions with nofralloc to avoid compiler-generated stack frame code for register context save/restore routines.
__setjmp saves LR via mflr, CR via mfcr, SP, RTOC, r13-r31 via stmw, FPSCR via mffs, and fp14-fp31/fpscr via stfd before returning 0.
longjmp restores LR, CR, SP, RTOC, r13-r31, fp14-fp31, and FPSCR, then returns val unless val is 0, in which case it returns 1.
Inline asm references C struct fields such as env->pc and env->fp31 to encode the required offsets through the recovered __jmp_buf layout.
The sequence preserves the original asm ordering, including loading fp0 from env->fpscr before fp31 and restoring FPSCR with mtfsf before returning.
follow_up_queries: Find other Runtime asm files that can be converted to MWERKS inline asm with recovered struct field offsets.
Search for remaining placeholder jump-buffer or setjmp-related types that should use __jmp_buf.
Check whether hsd_804D2648 uses now benefit from the concrete __jmp_buf layout.
Audit build metadata pairs when moving asm objects to src objects: configure.py and obj_files.mk.
